summary: Iteration 3 polish-pass regression + new-checks: backend 9/9 pytest passing (no API changes); frontend 30+ checks passing across regression and 5 NEW iter-3 assertions. All 8 home sections render in correct order (site-header, hero-section, proof-strip, featured-work, capability-pillars, selected-journey, recruiter-fit, contact-cta). All CV touchpoints (header, hero, footer, about, cta, mobile, contact) are <button type='button' disabled> with no <a href='#'> anchors. ZERO mailto: links anywhere; the email ahmed.ha.mahmoud@outlook.com never appears in visible DOM. LinkedIn URL https://www.linkedin.com/in/ahmed-mohsen-hanafy/ wired in header / footer / about / cta / contact. 404 page renders for unknown route with not-found-page + not-found-home. Capability pillars: pillar 1 open by default, click pillar 2 sets aria-expanded=true, click again closes (line indicator change verified via aria, not icons, per request). Recruiter FAQ: 5 items present, accordion toggles aria-expanded. Theme toggle adds .dark class on html. Mobile menu @ 390px opens, exposes mobile-download-cv (disabled <button>) and theme-toggle-button. Featured project card click navigates to /projects/:slug. Contact form POST /api/contact returns 201, fields reset, Sonner success toast 'Message sent — I'll reply within 48 hours' shown. PostHog analytics fire: hero_view_projects_clicked (source=hero), linkedin_clicked (source=header), contact_submit + contact_submit_success. NEW iter-3 — featured-cta-all-projects exists with text 'Browse all projects'; faq-cta-contact exists with text 'Have a different question? Ask'; hero-cta-projects keyboard focus-visible state computed outline=2px solid rgb(200,97,66) terracotta with offset (verified via Tab navigation, not .focus()); hero-subheadline class includes 'text-foreground/80'; FeaturedWork cards inline transitionDelay = ['0ms','140ms','280ms']. NEW touch-targets @ 390px: mobile-menu-toggle 44px, hero-cta-projects 48px, hero-cta-cv 46px, theme-toggle-button (inside mobile menu) 44px, cta-contact-page 68px — all >= 44px.
critical_code_review_comments: Contact-success surfacing: success state is shown via Sonner toast only (text 'Message sent — I'll reply within 48 hours') and form reset; there is no [data-testid="contact-success"] anchor element. Adding a data-testid on the toast (or rendering an inline aria-live success node) would make automated regression simpler going forward — minor DX/test-affordance, not a user bug.

header-linkedin testid in code is `header-linkedin-link` (anchor suffix); other linkedin testids are `footer-linkedin`, `about-linkedin`, `cta-linkedin`, `contact-linkedin`. Naming is slightly inconsistent — consider standardising to `<placement>-linkedin` everywhere.

Iter-2 carry-over (still valid): rate-limit window check is implemented via `db.contact_messages.count_documents({_ip, created_at >= window_start})` per request — fine at portfolio scale, but a sparse compound index `{_ip:1, created_at:-1}` is recommended before any traffic.
Iter-2 carry-over: created_at stored as ISO string and compared lexicographically — works for UTC 'Z' / '+00:00' but fragile if mixed offsets are ever introduced. Consider native datetime.

Iter-2 carry-over: PostHog public token is hard-coded in /app/frontend/public/index.html — public-safe, but a fork would attribute analytics to Ahmed's workspace; worth env-injecting before public source release.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all regression + iter-3 checks passing once selector mismatches were corrected (success toast vs testid, journey testid is selected-journey, focus-visible requires Tab not .focus())

seed_data_creation: Created during pytest: 1 TEST_status_*, 1 TEST_Recruiter, 1 TEST_BotHoneypot (not persisted), 5 TEST_RL_*. Created during Playwright: 2 TEST_Iter3_* contact submissions. CLEANED UP at end: db.contact_messages.deleteMany({}) -> 0 docs, db.status_checks.deleteMany({client_name:/^TEST_/}) -> 0 docs. Rate-limit budget for runner IP is fully reset.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-3 is a polish pass — backend is unchanged from iter-2, all 9 pytest still green at https://ahmed-analytics-eu.preview.emergentagent.com/api. Key gotchas for next agent: (a) Journey section testid is `selected-journey`, not `journey`; (b) header LinkedIn anchor testid is `header-linkedin-link` (suffix `-link`), other places drop the suffix; (c) contact form success is a Sonner toast — there is no `[data-testid="contact-success"]` element, assert via API 201 + form reset + toast text; (d) :focus-visible only triggers on keyboard input — programmatic .focus() will NOT trigger the terracotta outline, must use page.keyboard.press('Tab') after clicking a non-focusable element; (e) theme-toggle-button on mobile is INSIDE the mobile menu — there are TWO instances in DOM (desktop hidden via md:flex + mobile inside menu), use `is_visible()` or open the mobile menu first; (f) backend rate-limit is per-IP per 60min for 5 successful POSTs — when running pytest then Playwright contact submit back-to-back from same runner IP, run `mongosh test_database --eval 'db.contact_messages.deleteMany({})'` between phases (the rate-limit query reads from this collection); (g) pytest needs `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL` env var set (not loaded from .env automatically). Pillar indicator is now a custom CSS line — DO NOT search for Plus/Minus icons; use aria-expanded on `[data-testid="pillar-toggle-{research|campaign|data}"]`.
rca of the issue: n/a — no functional issues found. Minor selector/test mismatches were corrected during testing: (1) script tested for testid `journey` but actual is `selected-journey`; (2) script used `el.focus()` for focus-visible test which only triggers `:focus` (no outline) — switched to keyboard Tab and got the expected 2px solid terracotta outline rgb(200,97,66) with offset; (3) script asserted on `[data-testid="contact-success"]` element but success uses Sonner toast — verified instead via XHR 201 + visible toast text + form reset; (4) one Playwright contact submit hit 429 because pytest rate-limit test had used the IP budget — reset by deleting db.contact_messages and re-submitted successfully (201). All these are test-script artefacts, not app bugs.
